MELItem.Title: Turbine Case Cooling Air Flow Systems (GE)

ListItem.Para: Inoperative HPTACC valve is locked in the closed position.
Associated engine CCC valve is locked in the open position.
Associated engine LPTACC valve operates normally.
Both EECs operate in the normal mode.
Appropriate takeoff thrust settings are applied.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Lock the inoperative HPTACC valve in the closed position and the associated engine Core Compartment Cooling (CCC) valve open (AMM DDG 75-24-01).

ListItem.Para: Retract and deactivate the leading edge slats.
Deactivate the associated engine thrust reverser for ground maintenance.
Gain access to the HPTACC valve.
Lock the inoperative HPTACC valve in the closed position.
Gain access to the associated CCC valve.
Lock the associated CCC valve in the open position.
Activate the associated engine thrust reverser after ground maintenance.
Activate the leading edge slats.

OperationProcedure.rev:revHighlight: Updated performance penalty with additional procedural restriction to mitigate potential EGT exceedance events

ListItem.Para: Reduce performance limited weights as follows:

Para.Emphasis: Takeoff Field Length, Climb, and Obstacle Clearance

Note.Para: The enroute climb weight penalties are based on single engine operating speeds that approximate maximum lift-to-drag ratio speed. To account for the difference in level-off altitude when operating at other speeds, multiply the enroute climb weight penalty listed above by the appropriate factor listed on page 3.00-01-00.2 in the ENROUTE DIVERSION SPEED EFFECTS paragraph.

ListItem.Para: Increase flight planning fuel by either using a fuel flow correction factor of 1.3% or increase the flight planning fuel by 1.7% for each inoperative valve.

Full rated takeoff thrust is not allowed. Reduce full rated takeoff thrust by using either derated takeoff thrust of 3% or more, or reducing thrust with an assumed temperature of the greater of 6 degree C above OAT or 6 degree C above the thrust break temperature.
Reduce the all-engine, thrust limited maximum altitude by 2,600 feet to protect EGT margins.
Reduce performance limited weights as follows:

Para.Emphasis: Takeoff Field Length, Climb, and Obstacle Clearance

Note.Para: The enroute climb weight penalties are based on single engine operating speeds that approximate maximum lift-to-drag ratio speed. To account for the difference in level-off altitude when operating at other speeds, multiply the enroute climb weight penalty listed above by the appropriate factor listed on page 3.00-01-00.2 in the ENROUTE DIVERSION SPEED EFFECTS paragraph.

ListItem.Para: Increase flight planning fuel by either using a fuel flow correction factor of 1.3% or increase the flight planning fuel by 1.6% for each inoperative valve.

Full rated takeoff thrust is not allowed. Reduce full rated takeoff thrust by using either derated takeoff thrust of 3% or more, or reducing thrust with an assumed temperature of the greater of 6 degree C above OAT or 6 degree C above the thrust break temperature.
Reduce the all-engine, thrust-limited maximum altitude by 4,700 feet to protect EGT margins.
Inoperative LPTACC valve is locked in the closed position.
Associated engine CCC valve is locked in the open position.
Associated engine HPTACC valve operates normally.
Both EECs operate in the normal mode.
Appropriate takeoff thrust settings are applied.
Appropriate performance adjustments are applied.

MaintenanceProcedure.Para: Lock the inoperative LPTACC valve in the closed position and lock the associated engine Core Compartment Cooling (CCC) valve open (AMM DDG 75-24-01).

ListItem.Para: Retract and deactivate the leading edge slats.
Deactivate the associated engine thrust reverser for ground maintenance.
Gain access to the LPTACC valve.
Lock the inoperative LPTACC valve in the closed position.
Gain access to the associated CCC valve.
Lock the associated CCC valve in the open position.
Activate the associated engine thrust reverser after ground maintenance.
Activate the leading edge slats.

OperationProcedure.rev:revHighlight: Updated performance penalty with additional procedural restriction to mitigate potential EGT exceedance events

ListItem.Para: Use OPT to determine performance weight without impacted MEL (do not select impacted MEL in OPT)
Then directly reduce performance limited weights as follows:

Para.Emphasis: Takeoff Field Length, Climb, and Obstacle Clearance

Note.Para: The enroute climb weight penalties are based on single engine operating speeds that approximate maximum lift-to-drag ratio speed. To account for the difference in level-off altitude when operating at other speeds, multiply the enroute climb weight penalty listed above by the appropriate factor listed on page 3.00-01-00.2 in the ENROUTE DIVERSION SPEED EFFECTS paragraph.

ListItem.Para: Increase flight planning fuel by either using a fuel flow correction factor of 0.6% or increase the flight planning fuel by 0.8% for each inoperative valve.
For ETOPS flight planning, increase engine inoperative Critical Fuel Reserves by 1.1%

Full rated takeoff thrust is not allowed. Reduce full rated takeoff thrust by using either derated takeoff thrust of 2% or more, or reducing thrust with an assumed temperature of the greater of 6 degree C above OAT or 6 degree C above the thrust break temperature.
Reduce performance limited weights as follows:

Para.Emphasis: Takeoff Field Length, Climb, and Obstacle Clearance

Note.Para: The enroute climb weight penalties are based on single engine operating speeds that approximate maximum lift-to-drag ratio speed. To account for the difference in level-off altitude when operating at other speeds, multiply the enroute climb weight penalty listed above by the appropriate factor listed on page 3.00-01-00.2 in the ENROUTE DIVERSION SPEED EFFECTS paragraph.

ListItem.Para: Increase flight planning fuel by either using a fuel flow correction factor of 0.6% or increase the flight planning fuel by 0.7% for each inoperative valve.
For ETOPS flight planning, increase engine inoperative Critical Fuel Reserves by 1.1%.

Full rated takeoff thrust is not allowed. Reduce full rated takeoff thrust by using either derated takeoff thrust of 2% or more, or reducing thrust with an assumed temperature of the greater of 6 degree C above OAT or 6 degree C above the thrust break temperature.
